lists.title: Art History & Classics
lists.description: A curated reading path aligned with the Art History/Classics Library — home to the Fine Arts Collection (200,000+ volumes) and the Classics Collection covering all aspects of ancient Greco-Roman civilization. Supports research in History of Art, Art Practice, Classics, Ancient History, and Mediterranean Archaeology.

collections.name: Classics Collection
collections.focus: Greco-Roman texts, philology, ancient history, classical philosophy, site reports, and legacy cultures from Byzantium to early Christianity.

collections.name: Fine Arts Collection
collections.focus: Ancient through contemporary art, exhibition catalogs, catalogue raisonnés, and visual culture across Europe, Asia, and the Americas.

sections.title: Epic Foundations
sections.description: The philological core — Homeric and Virgilian epic that anchors Berkeley's classical studies.

sections.title: Greek Drama & Tragedy
sections.description: Stage conflict, fate, and civic identity — from Aeschylus to Aristophanes.

sections.title: Classical Philosophy
sections.description: Platonic dialogue and Stoic practice — how Greeks and Romans thought about virtue, love, and the good life.

sections.title: Ancient Historians
sections.description: Primary accounts of war, empire, and the invention of history as a discipline.

sections.title: Myth & Literary Reception
sections.description: Ovidian transformation and modern retellings — how classical myth travels across centuries.

sections.title: Art History & Visual Theory
sections.description: Foundational criticism on seeing, reproduction, and photography — essential for History of Art coursework.

sections.title: Artists, Patrons & Lives
sections.description: Biography, fiction, and Vasari's Renaissance lens on how artists are remembered.

sections.title: Archaeology & Material Culture
sections.description: Sites, ruins, and rediscovery — from Pompeii to Mediterranean excavation.

sections.title: Modern Encounters with Antiquity
sections.description: 20th-century novels and essays that argue with the classical past.
